codeReview.summary: The broadcast propagation fix looks sound, but the required live transport regression is still only simulated through helper calls. The commit never actually drives handler.ServeHTTP or session.CallTool, so VAL-SUPERVISOR-003 remains unproven on the production path.

issues.description: The existing 'TestStreamableHTTPHandlerIntegration' test still only calls GetServerForRequest/GetServerInstance and inspects CreatedBy. It never invokes handler.ServeHTTP or session.CallTool, so the claimed production StreamableHTTP path is not exercised.

The new 'TestStreamableHTTPLiveTransportProvenance' test repeats the same helper-only pattern: it constructs a handler, but only uses GetServerForRequest/GetServerInstance and CreatedBy/Actor checks. No live ServeHTTP or CallTool invocation occurs, so the required end-to-end proof is still missing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file now contains multiple comments that describe an 'actual StreamableHTTP handler path' while the code only exercises helper-level request selection. That naming pattern can mislead future reviewers into accepting synthetic coverage as live transport proof.

The control-plane-worker guidance should explicitly require a verified ServeHTTP or session.CallTool invocation when a feature claims live transport coverage; this transcript and handoff treat GetServerForRequest as sufficient, which is not enough for the stated contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff claims the production StreamableHTTP path is proven, but the test bodies only call GetServerForRequest/GetServerInstance at internal/mcpserver/server_streamablehttp_test.go:284-450

summary: FAIL: broadcast propagation was fixed, but the fix still does not prove the live transport path end-to-end.
